channel.title: Gortex on Andrey Kumanyaev
channel.description: Recent content in Gortex on Andrey Kumanyaev

item.title: Retrieval got a lot smarter
item.description: How Gortex turned code retrieval from tuned-by-feel into measured-and-ranked — graph-centrality reranking, per-query lexical/semantic blending, query understanding, and a real P@K/R@K/MRR evaluation harness.

item.title: smart_context: less, but more relevant
item.description: smart_context packs the smallest correct working set for a task: dependency-closure selection, a budget that scales with the graph, fidelity tiers with compress_bodies, and delta packing that re-sends only what changed.

item.title: Cross-language resolution & framework awareness
item.description: A code graph is only as good as its edges. A framework dynamic-dispatch synthesizer engine recovers the cross-language and cross-framework call edges that static parsing alone misses.

item.title: Many more languages — and file types
item.description: Gortex now ingests infrastructure-as-code, legacy systems, docs, images, PDFs, and live database schemas into one graph — plus two config-driven SPIs to add your own formats without a fork.

item.title: Deeper analysis
item.description: Push hard codebase questions into the graph: an interprocedural bottleneck analyzer for hidden hot paths that compound across calls, and first-class capability edges that make a least-privilege audit a one-hop query.

item.title: A much better teammate for coding agents
item.description: Two memory layers, a multi-agent coordination registry, and a preview-and-verify edit gate make Gortex a safe, productive teammate for autonomous coding agents over MCP.

item.title: LLM providers & graph-aware routing
item.description: Make the model a configuration choice, not a code dependency: one provider interface, nine interchangeable LLM backends, and graph-aware routing by task complexity.

item.title: A rebuilt storage & performance layer
item.description: A rebuilt storage layer — a pure-Go SQLite backend by default (no CGO, FTS5 search), sidecars that keep agent knowledge off the hot path, warm-restart reconcile in seconds, and graph walks pushed into indexed Store capabilities.

item.title: Multi-workspace & git worktrees
item.description: Gortex tracks each git worktree as an independent repository instance with its own graph, so usages, callers, and resolution stay correctly isolated per checkout.

item.title: Code search for AI agents: the grep replacement is three tools, not one
item.description: Code search for AI agents needs three modalities, not one: lexical, structural, and graph. A field guide to when grep, ast-grep, and a code graph each win.

item.title: Plain-English guide: what changed, and how to use it
item.description: A plain-English tour of the May–June 2026 Gortex updates for the engineers who use it: what each change means in practice, plus the exact commands and settings to get the benefit — no graph theory required.

item.title: What's new in Gortex — May–June 2026
item.description: A month of code-intelligence upgrades across Gortex v0.19 to v0.39: smarter retrieval, wider language ingest, deeper analysis, better agent ergonomics, and a rebuilt storage layer.

item.title: Self-Hosted Code Intelligence for AI Agents: Local-First, No Cloud
item.description: Self-hosted code intelligence for AI agents that runs offline: air-gapped, no API key, Apache 2.0. How local code graphs compare and where Gortex fits.

item.title: The MCP server for codebase context you're missing
item.description: Pick an MCP server for codebase context: a fair rubric scoring claude-context, Serena, codanna, and Context7, and why a graph-native server wins.

item.title: Rename a function across files safely: graph-verified refactoring
item.description: Rename a function across files safely, find dead code, and detect circular dependencies with a code graph instead of grep-sed or a guessing AI agent.

item.title: How does Cursor index your codebase? Embeddings vs a graph
item.description: How does Cursor index your codebase: the real chunk-embed-Merkle pipeline, where it misses, and how a structural graph resolves edges over similarity.

item.title: Repomix Alternative for AI Agents: Query a Graph, Don't Concatenate
item.description: A Repomix alternative for AI agents: why concatenating a repo blows the token budget, when packers still win, and how a code graph fetches only the working set.

item.title: Find usages without grep false positives
item.description: Find usages without grep false positives: why grep matches comments and strings, the ast-grep/LSP/code-graph ladder, and zero-false-positive references.

item.title: Impact analysis: what breaks if I change this function?
item.description: Impact analysis: what breaks if I change this function? The honest blast-radius menu — tests, LSP, grep — and a sub-millisecond pre-edit gate for AI agents.

item.title: Your codebase is too large for the context window
item.description: Codebase too large for context window? You can't paste a monorepo, and bigger windows rot. The fix is the right minimal working set, selected on demand.

item.title: Codanna alternative? Local code-graph MCP servers compared
item.description: A codanna alternative comparison: codanna, ChunkHound, Serena, Context7 and Gortex side by side — graph vs embeddings vs LSP vs docs, by use case.

item.title: Graph RAG vs vector RAG for code
item.description: Graph RAG vs vector RAG for code: an honest, code-specific breakdown of embeddings, resolved edges, why agents reach for grep, and the hybrid RRF answer.

item.title: Code Knowledge Graph for LLM Agents: What It Is and Why You Need One
item.description: A code knowledge graph for LLM agents: a crisp definition of nodes and edges, graph vs grep vs vector, and how to index a repository into a graph.

item.title: Taint analysis sources and sinks: SAST from the code graph
item.description: Taint analysis sources and sinks, explained — plus how a code property graph runs source-to-sink and hardcoded-secret checks as an agent-edit-time guardrail.

item.title: Call graph for an AI coding assistant: who calls this?
item.description: A call graph for AI coding assistant work: why grep can't trace callers, how code2flow/codanna compare, and resolved cross-language call chains.

item.title: Sourcegraph alternative self-hosted: a Cody replacement for AI agents
item.description: A Sourcegraph alternative self-hosted for AI agents: how OpenGrok, Zoekt, Greptile, and an Apache-2.0 code graph compare on cost, ops, and MCP.

item.title: Stop AI agent hallucinating code: a context problem
item.description: How to stop AI agent hallucinating code: why agents invent functions that don't exist, and how grounding generation in your repo's real symbols reduces it.

item.title: Cursor not understanding my codebase? Bolt on a graph
item.description: Cursor not understanding my codebase? Cline missing references? Keep your tool and add a code-graph MCP server so the agent queries edges, not guesses.

item.title: Why AI coding agents fail in large codebases
item.description: Why AI coding agents fail in large codebases: context windows can't hold a monorepo, flattening kills the call graph, and bigger windows rot. Here's the fix.

item.title: Token Efficient Code Retrieval for AI Agents: Stop Wasting Tokens Finding Code
item.description: Token efficient code retrieval for AI agents: why Claude Code is so expensive, the honest fix menu, and how a code graph cuts agent token waste 3–50×.

item.title: Context engineering for coding agents, automated
item.description: Context engineering for coding agents, explained: the working-set and just-in-time-retrieval framing, and how a code graph automates the retrieval slice.

item.title: Anthropic's large codebase guide is a Gortex tutorial in disguise
item.description: Anthropic published a best-practices guide for Claude Code in large codebases. It's a good guide. It also describes, step by step, exactly what Gortex ships out of the box — and reveals the real cost of the grep-based approach it defends.

item.title: Code intelligence tools compared — the missing row
item.description: A response to rywalker.com/research/code-intelligence-tools, which surveys the code-intelligence landscape and names GitNexus the Tier-1 leader. The article is solid. It also doesn't evaluate Gortex, which directly contradicts three of its headline findings.

item.title: Gortex: Week 2 — DI graph, 3D web UI, dependency graph, enrichment, SQL, and 50 MCP tools
item.description: Two weeks of shipping since the last post: DI framework support (NestJS/FastAPI/Spring/Laravel/Rails), react-three-fiber 3D web UI, gortex eval CLI, external package graph, gortex enrich blame+coverage, six new analyzers, deep Go graph, SQL migration analysis, and feature flags.

item.title: GCX1 — a compact wire format for MCP tool responses
item.description: Median −27.4% tiktoken savings vs JSON, 100% round-trip integrity, across 20 representative MCP tool responses. Shipped, stable, reference decoders in Go and TypeScript.

item.title: Gortex: What Changed and How It Compares to Alternatives
item.description: A follow-up on Gortex after a week of heavy development: 92 languages, daemon mode, GCX1 wire format, LSP edge tiers, cumulative cost tracking — plus an honest comparison with repomix, Cursor's built-in indexing, GitNexus, and Sourcegraph.

item.title: From GitNexus to Gortex: Building a Go Code Intelligence Engine
item.description: How I experimented with GitNexus, discovered its limitations, and built Gortex — a Go code intelligence engine with 48 MCP tools, 33-language support, inline symbol editing, API contract detection, multi-repo mode, and measurable 94%+ token reduction for AI agents.
